message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  In this randomized, observer-blinded, phase 2/3 study, S-268019-b (n=101), a recombinant spike protein vaccine, was analyzed for noninferiority versus tozinameran (n=103), when given as a booster ≥6 months after 2-dose tozinameran regimen in Japanese adults without prior COVID-19 infection. Interim results showed noninferiority of S-268019-b versus tozinameran in co-primary endpoints for neutralizing antibodies on day 29: geometric mean titer (GMT) (124.97 versus 109.70; adjusted-GMT ratio [95% CI], 1.14 [0.94-1.39]; noninferiority
                  <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                  -value, <0.0001) and seroresponse rate (both 100%; noninferiority
                  <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                  -value, 0.0004). Both vaccines elicited anti-spike-protein immunoglobulin G antibodies, and produced T-cell response (n=29/group) and neutralizing antibodies against Delta and Omicron pseudovirus and live virus variants (n=24/group) in subgroups. Most participants reported low-grade reactogenicity on days 1-2, the most frequent being fatigue, fever, myalgia, and injection-site pain. No serious adverse events were reported. In conclusion, S-268019-b was safe and showed robust immunogenicity as a booster, supporting its use as COVID-19 booster vaccine.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>JRCT ID</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>jRCT2031210470</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Third COVID-19 vaccine dose (booster) enhances immune response</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Interim phase 2/3 data for booster ≥6 months after the 2nd dose in Japan are shown</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>S-268019-b was noninferior to tozinameran in inducing neutralizing antibodies</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Sera boosted with either vaccines neutralized Delta and Omicron virus variants</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>S-268019-b was safe, and results support its use as a booster in vaccinated adults</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Immunogenicity and Safety of Booster Dose of S-268019-b or Tozinameran in Japanese Participants: An Interim Report of Phase 2/3, Randomized, Observer-Blinded, Noninferiority Study
